items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.

items.q: Why did Henry II clash with Thomas Becket?
items.correct: Becket resisted Henry's attempt to bring 'criminous clerks' under royal courts via the Constitutions of Clarendon, defending Church independence from royal jurisdiction.
items.misconception: Henry II ordered Becket's assassination personally.
items.random_wrong: They quarrelled because Becket wanted to be Pope.
items.why: Henry's outburst was rhetorical and he did penance after. The dispute was about jurisdiction over clergy, not personal hatred — students dramatise the murder and miss the constitutional issue.

items.q: What did Magna Carta actually do in 1215?
items.correct: It was a peace treaty between King John and rebel barons protecting baronial privileges and limiting arbitrary royal taxation and imprisonment — it was annulled within months.
items.misconception: Magna Carta gave ordinary English people democratic rights and freedom.
items.random_wrong: Magna Carta abolished the monarchy and created Parliament.
items.why: Magna Carta protected the elite, not commoners; villeins are barely mentioned. Its democratic reputation is a later (17th-century) reinterpretation projected backwards.
The answer examiners want is: Gulf of Tonkin. “Bay of Pigs” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What was the peak US troop level in Vietnam?
items.why: Correct answer: 540,000. “50,000” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was the Tet Offensive?
items.why: The real figure is 1968. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
